summary: Updated the vendored m2c tooling with a small upstream-style maintenance pass: removed unused imports/locals, added a --force-decimal formatter option, adjusted global declaration sort ordering, fixed union-vs-struct CType emission for anonymous/tagged struct-union types, and changed asm label parsing so jlabel is treated as an in-function jump target rather than a global/function label. The weird-asm end-to-end test was extended to cover jlabel behavior.
what_changed: Added an m2c CLI flag --force-decimal in main.py and threaded it into Options.force_decimal and Formatter.force_decimal.

Changed Formatter.format_int so force_decimal causes all integer constants to be emitted in decimal form; previously only values with abs(val) < 10 used decimal before falling back to hex formatting behavior.
Changed asm_file.py label handling: process_label now accepts Optional[bool] for glabel, plain colon labels pass glabel=None, and directives pass glabel=(directive != "jlabel").

Changed .text label classification so labels starting with '.' or labels explicitly marked with glabel=False are treated as local in-function labels. This makes jlabel create a jump label inside the current function instead of being treated like glabel/dlabel.
Extended tools/m2c/tests/end_to_end/weird-asm/test.s with a branch to jumptarget_label followed by jlabel jumptarget_label; updated expected output warning from .label to .jumptarget_label.
Fixed Type.to_ctype-related handling in types.py to create ca.Union when data.struct.is_union is true and ca.Struct otherwise, instead of always constructing ca.Struct.
Adjusted translate.py global_decls sorting so is_global is only considered when the symbol is not in-file: is_global if not is_in_file else False.
Removed many unused imports and unused locals across arch_mips.py, arch_ppc.py, c_types.py, demangle_codewarrior.py, evaluate.py, flow_graph.py, ir_pattern.py, main.py, translate.py, and types.py.
Applied minor style simplifications such as replacing f-strings without interpolations with plain string literals and using 'curr_start not in context.emitted_nodes'.
smart_moves: The jlabel change is narrow and explicit: it reuses the existing process_label path but distinguishes glabel/dlabel from jlabel via a tri-state Optional[bool], avoiding a larger parser rewrite.
The weird-asm end-to-end test directly exercises the new jlabel semantics by adding a branch target and asserting the changed diagnostic target name.
The --force-decimal feature was threaded through the same Options-to-Formatter path as existing zfill_constants behavior, keeping numeric formatting policy centralized in Formatter.format_int.
The union CType fix constructs the pycparser ca.Union or ca.Struct object before wrapping it in ca.TypeDecl, preserving the existing TypeDecl interface while correcting the underlying kind.
Most cleanup changes remove unused imports/locals without changing logic, reducing type-checker/linter noise in a vendored decompilation helper.
decomp_lessons: For m2c asm parsing, directive names matter: glabel/dlabel and jlabel should not necessarily imply the same label scope. jlabel can represent a local jump target within a function.
When adding m2c output-formatting knobs, thread the option from argparse to Options and then to Formatter rather than scattering formatting decisions through translation code.
Type emission code must preserve struct-vs-union identity even for anonymous or tag-only types; using ca.Struct unconditionally can silently produce the wrong C model.
End-to-end tests for unusual assembly syntax are valuable because small parser classification changes can alter function boundaries, label names, and emitted diagnostics.
Cleanup of unused imports and locals can be a useful part of m2c updates because the tool spans many architecture, IR, type, and formatting modules.
naming_conventions: Options and Formatter fields use snake_case for the same setting: force_decimal.
Existing label directives distinguished in parser logic include glabel, dlabel, and jlabel.
The weird-asm test uses jumptarget_label as the jlabel target name, and expected diagnostics show it as .jumptarget_label.
Internal synthetic conditional-return target remains named _m2c_conditionalreturn_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Treat jlabel as glabel=False when processing .text labels so it becomes a local label associated with the current function.
Plain colon labels in asm_file.py now pass glabel=None, separating implicit labels from directive-driven global/local label intent.
In .text, labels starting with '.' or labels with glabel explicitly false are routed to asm_file.new_label(label.lstrip('.')) and require an active current_function.
The weird-asm regression covers a branch to jumptarget_label followed by jlabel jumptarget_label to ensure m2c recognizes it as a jump target rather than a new function/global.
No direct decompilation matching tactic for Melee C functions is evidenced; this PR is focused on m2c tooling behavior.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching tools/m2c/m2c/asm_file.py jlabel handling to see whether the Optional[bool] glabel distinction was refined.
Find uses of --force-decimal or force_decimal in project scripts to understand when decimal constants were preferred for Melee decomp output.
Search later m2c updates for ca.Union or StructUnion fixes to see whether more struct-vs-union CType issues were found.
Check weird-asm test history for additional unusual label/directive parser regressions.
